vocabulary.definition: Something that can grow, move, breathe, and needs food
A living thing that grows from soil and makes its own food using sunlight
A living thing that can move around and usually eats other living things for food
The natural home where a plant or animal lives
What living things eat to have energy and stay healthy
A tiny part of a plant that can grow into a new plant

questions: What makes something a living thing?
How are plants and animals the same? How are they different?
What do plants need to grow?
What do animals need to survive?
Why do different animals live in different places?
Can you name three living things in your backyard?
What happens to a plant when it gets too little water or sunlight?

activities.name: Nature Walk
activities.description: Take a walk outside and identify living things. Count how many different plants and animals you can spot.

activities.name: Seed Planting
activities.description: Plant a bean seed in a small cup. Watch it grow over several weeks and record changes.

activities.name: Animal Classification
activities.description: Sort pictures of animals into groups: pets vs. wild animals, or animals that fly vs. animals that walk.

activities.name: Food Chain Story
activities.description: Create a simple story showing how a plant gets energy from the sun, and an animal eats the plant.

activities.name: Bug Detective
activities.description: Search for insects in the garden. Observe them for a few minutes and discuss what they are doing.

activities.name: Animal Habitat Art
activities.description: Draw or build a habitat for your favorite animal using craft supplies or drawings.
